relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Exploitative wealth is summoned to weep.
Rot and moth expose false security.
Stored treasure testifies against hoarding.
Withheld wages cry out to the Lord of hosts.
Luxury built on harm becomes judgment.
The righteous one is condemned by oppressive power.
Patience waits like the farmer for rain and fruit.
Establish your hearts; the Lord’s arrival has drawn near.
Do not groan against siblings; the Judge stands near.
The prophets become examples of patient endurance.
Job’s endurance reveals the Lord’s compassion and mercy.
Let yes be yes and no be no.
Suffering prays; cheer sings.
The weak are gathered into prayer and anointing.
Prayer of trust seeks healing, raising, and release.
Confession and prayer open healing among one another.
Elijah prayed as a human like us.
Prayer and heaven’s rain bring fruit from earth.
A wandering sibling may be turned back.
Turning one back from wandering saves life and covers much misalignment.

contemplative_note: James 5 is justice and healing: wages cry, hearts wait, the weak are anointed, and wandering souls can be brought home.
